arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta lifts lid on Stoke City comparisons
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has said it is a “big compliment” to be compared to Tony Pulis’ Stoke City side and said Arsenal want to be the “kings of everything” as he moved to defend the club’s set-piece record. Arsenal’s set-piece guru Nicolas Jover, who Arteta first took to Manchester City and then with him to Arsenal, has been the figurehead behind the club’s dead-ball success.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta responds to Dimitar Berbatov comparing Arsenal to Stoke
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta responds to comparisons between his Arsenal side and Stoke City.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta insists Arsenal take Dimitar Berbatov's Stoke City comparisons as a 'compliment' as he says his team want to be 'the kings of everything' and not just corners
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta insisted he is flattered by comparisons to Stoke City over Arsenal's set-piece prowess - and is aiming to dominate across all areas of the game.

arts.ttl: Cirkin returns: Sunderland team news and predicted XI to face Stoke City
arts.sum: The latest Sunderland team news as Regis Le Bris' side gear up to face Stoke City in the Championship. The post Cirkin returns: Sunderland team news and predicted XI to face Stoke City appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Arsenal are like Pulis-era Stoke? Thanks for the compliment, says Mikel Arteta
arts.sum: Arsenal manager proud of his side’s set-piece record ‘We want to the best at everything,’ says Arteta Mikel Arteta has said it is a compliment for Arsenal to be compared to Stoke under Tony Pulis for their set-piece prowess but warned their rivals that they still have room to improve in that area. Arsenal scored twice from corners in Wednesday’s win against Manchester United to take their tally to 22 goals via set pieces since the start of last season. Their total of 16 in the previous campaign matched the Premier League record set by West Brom under the management of Pulis in 2016-17, with the former Tottenham striker Dimitar Berbatov joking that Arsenal are the “new Stoke City” while working as a pundit for their win over United. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta claims comparisons with Stoke are a ‘big compliment’ for Arsenal
arts.sum: Arsenal have been likened to Tony Pulis’ Stoke side due to their impressive proficiency from corners

arts.ttl: 'Arsenal are the new Stoke City' - Tony Pulis issues vindicated response
arts.sum: Tony Pulis has issued a vindicated response after Arsenal were dubbed “the new Stoke City ” by former Manchester United forward Dimitar Berbatov. The Gunners have scored right set piece goals in the Premier League so far this season, the joint-most of any club, including two during their win over Man Utd at the Emirates Stadium on Wednesday night.

arts.ttl: 'Big' Stoke City strive to regain Arsenal crown as new weapon unearthed
arts.sum: Narcis Pelach stopped short of saying he wants Stoke City to steal back the set piece crown taken by Mikel Arteta’s Arsenal but he is hammering home his big belief in dead ball expertise – including adding a long throw. I think we need to find the right profile for Stoke City but it’s something I look after, I want presence and I want to be big because I know at set plays we can be better when we defend and when we attack.

arts.ttl: Arteta welcomes Stoke comparisons: 'We want to be kings of everything'
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta insists he is flattered by comparisons to Stoke City over Arsenal's set-piece prowess and says the Gunners are aiming to become the best in all areas of the game.

arts.ttl: 'The penny has dropped' - Tony Pulis responds as Arsenal branded 'the new Stoke City'
arts.sum: Arsenal scored both of their goals during Wednesday's 2-0 win at home to Manchester United from corners, prompting comparisons between Mikel Arteta's side and Tony Pulis' Stoke City
